qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DoS via missing HTTP timeout in custom notification handler
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding targets changedetectionio/notification/apprise_plugin/custom_handlers.py requests.request() without a timeout, not the advisory's html_tools.py xpath_filter() path using XPath3Parser and unparsed-text() for file reads.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ReDoS via user-controlled regex patterns in extract endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding targets user-controlled regex execution through /diff/<uuid>/extract and re.findall()/finditer() catastrophic backtracking, not the advisory's XPath include_filters flow to elementpath.select() with unparsed-text().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described arbitrary file read root cause: user-supplied include_filters XPath reaching changedetectionio/html_tools.py:xpath_filter(), elementpath.select(..., parser=XPath3Parser), and XPath 3 unparsed-text() without function safety validation.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6fmw-82m7-jq6p describes arbitrary file read via XPath include_filters and unparsed-text() in changedetectionio/html_tools.py:xpath_filter().
dos-via-missing-http-timeout/vulnerability_report.json describes a notification custom handler HTTP request without timeout causing worker availability impact.
redos-via-user-controlled-regex/vulnerability_report.json describes regex catastrophic backtracking in extract/history processing causing availability impact.
Neither qualifying report identifies XPath3Parser, elementpath.select(), unparsed-text(), include_filters XPath validation, or arbitrary file read through filtered content.
